description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from large, rounded timber logs, with a prominent gabled roof covered in dark shingles. The front facade features a covered porch with a peaked entryway supported by log columns, and a set of steps leading up to the main door. The exterior walls are composed of horizontally stacked logs, and the base of the structure is finished with a stone or masonry foundation. Multiple windows of varying sizes are distributed asymmetrically across the visible sides, with some grouped together and others standing alone. A stone chimney rises from one side of the roof, suggesting the presence of a fireplace inside. The overall design is robust and traditional, evoking a cozy, woodland retreat suitable for residential use.
description: A traditional log cabin with a dark gabled roof, covered porch, stone foundation, and asymmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entrance, and symmetrical gabled roof with visible log construction.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the log wall texture, the porch roof extension, and minimal window visibility.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying large windows, a secondary gable, and the stone foundation, with the chimney visible on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a large set of windows under the gable, the chimney, and the log wall details.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with intersecting gables, moderate overhangs, and dark asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Asymmetrical distribution with multiple rectangular windows of varying sizes; some grouped in pairs or clusters, others standalone, including a large set of windows on the rear gable
attribute_door_placement: Single main door centered on the front facade beneath the porch roof; no other exterior doors visible
